message:
Close DCG epic and Tier 1 Safety & Reliability epic

All Tier 1 integrations complete:
- caut: Usage tracking and quota visibility
- CAAM: Account switching for rate limit recovery
- DCG: Destructive command guard for agent safety

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2q3cg","title":"Task: Implement robot renderer interface (JSON + TOON)","description":"## Overview
Centralize robot output serialization behind a renderer so every command emits consistent output.

## Design
- `type RobotFormat string` {json, toon}
- `Render(payload any, format RobotFormat) (string, error)`
- Single entry point used by all JSON/TOON robot commands

## Compatibility
- `--robot-terse` (single‑line encoded state) bypasses renderer and remains unchanged.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All JSON/TOON robot outputs go through renderer
- JSON remains identical to current behavior
- Renderer returns content‑type hint for tooling","notes":"Decision linkage: renderer should call in-process Go TOON encoder (no CLI dependency). Auto format chooses JSON vs TOON based on payload suitability/size, not tool availability.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:14:09.538056742Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T03:09:49.636836502Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T03:09:49.636790195Z","close_reason":"Implemented robot renderer interface with:

- Renderer interface with Render(), ContentType(), Format() methods

- TOONRenderer: stub that returns clear error pointing to bd-4xgr6
- Render(payload, format) as single entry point for all robot commands
- Output/OutputTo helpers for writing to stdout/io.Writer
- RenderWithMeta for advanced use cases needing content-type metadata
- 17 comprehensive tests verifying backward compatibility with encodeJSON","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2q3cg","depends_on_id":"bd-1fmug","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:14:09.566388816Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2q3cg","depends_on_id":"bd-2csfi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:15:37.275325454Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2q3cg","depends_on_id":"bd-2h4bq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:15:44.086037846Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-2q9px","title":"DCG Integration: Destructive Command Guard for Agent Safety","description":"Integrate DCG (destructive_commit_guard) to block dangerous commands across all managed agents.

## Project Location
/dp/destructive_commit_guard (Rust CLI)

## What DCG Does

DCG is a whitelist-first pattern matcher that intercepts commands before execution and blocks known-dangerous patterns:
- `rm -rf /`, `rm -rf ~`, `rm -rf *`
- `git reset --hard`, `git push --force origin main`
- `DROP DATABASE`, `TRUNCATE TABLE`
- `kubectl delete namespace`, `terraform destroy`
- `chmod -R 777`, `chown -R` on sensitive paths

### Architecture
- Whitelist patterns: known-safe commands pass through
- Blocklist patterns: known-dangerous commands blocked
- Context-aware: understands git branch, current directory
- Override: `--i-know-what-im-doing` flag for explicit bypass

## Integration Strategy for NTM

### Option A: PreToolUse Hook (Recommended)
Claude Code agents support PreToolUse hooks that run before any Bash tool execution.
NTM can configure this hook for all spawned agents automatically.

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "PreToolUse": [{
      "matcher": "Bash",
      "command": "dcg check --command \"\""
    }]
  }
}
```

### Option B: Shell Wrapper
Wrap bash with dcg-guarded version, but this is more invasive.

### Option C: NTM Send Interception
Intercept `ntm send` commands and run through DCG before forwarding to pane.
Good for non-Claude agents (Codex, Gemini).

## Implementation Plan
1. Detect if dcg binary is available on PATH
2. Add config option: [integrations.dcg] enabled = true
3. For Claude agents: auto-configure PreToolUse hook
4. For other agents: intercept at ntm send level
5. Add --robot-dcg-status command to check DCG state
6. Dashboard indicator: 🛡️ when DCG active

## Considerations
- DCG must not slow down agent execution (< 10ms overhead)
- Override mechanism needed for legitimate destructive ops
- Logging: record all blocked commands for audit
- Need to handle dcg binary not found gracefully","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:43:55.251044280Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T06:43:55.288847766Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2q9px","depends_on_id":"bd-mk1i5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:43:55.288792692Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2q9px","title":"DCG Integration: Destructive Command Guard for Agent Safety","description":"Integrate DCG (destructive_commit_guard) to block dangerous commands across all managed agents.

## Project Location
/dp/destructive_commit_guard (Rust CLI)

## What DCG Does

DCG is a whitelist-first pattern matcher that intercepts commands before execution and blocks known-dangerous patterns:
- `rm -rf /`, `rm -rf ~`, `rm -rf *`
- `git reset --hard`, `git push --force origin main`
- `DROP DATABASE`, `TRUNCATE TABLE`
- `kubectl delete namespace`, `terraform destroy`
- `chmod -R 777`, `chown -R` on sensitive paths

### Architecture
- Whitelist patterns: known-safe commands pass through
- Blocklist patterns: known-dangerous commands blocked
- Context-aware: understands git branch, current directory
- Override: `--i-know-what-im-doing` flag for explicit bypass

## Integration Strategy for NTM

### Option A: PreToolUse Hook (Recommended)
Claude Code agents support PreToolUse hooks that run before any Bash tool execution.
NTM can configure this hook for all spawned agents automatically.

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "PreToolUse": [{
      "matcher": "Bash",
      "command": "dcg check --command \"\""
    }]
  }
}
```

### Option B: Shell Wrapper
Wrap bash with dcg-guarded version, but this is more invasive.

### Option C: NTM Send Interception
Intercept `ntm send` commands and run through DCG before forwarding to pane.
Good for non-Claude agents (Codex, Gemini).

## Implementation Plan
1. Detect if dcg binary is available on PATH
2. Add config option: [integrations.dcg] enabled = true
3. For Claude agents: auto-configure PreToolUse hook
4. For other agents: intercept at ntm send level
5. Add --robot-dcg-status command to check DCG state
6. Dashboard indicator: 🛡️ when DCG active

## Considerations
- DCG must not slow down agent execution (< 10ms overhead)
- Override mechanism needed for legitimate destructive ops
- Logging: record all blocked commands for audit
- Need to handle dcg binary not found gracefully","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:43:55.251044280Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:58:58.517130815Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:58:58.516640433Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2q9px","depends_on_id":"bd-mk1i5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:43:55.288792692Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qcn","title":"Improve text truncation to preserve useful information","description":"## Problem
Text truncation is too aggressive, cutting off useful information and showing many entries that look identical.

## Code Review Findings (2026-01-19)

### Confirmed: Simple End Truncation

**layout.go:71-83** - TruncateRunes just cuts from end:
```go
func TruncateRunes(s string, max int, suffix string) string {
    runes := []rune(s)
    if len(runes) <= max {
        return s
    }
    return string(runes[:max-len([]rune(suffix))]) + suffix  // Always cuts end
}
```

This produces:
```
destructive_command_guar...
destructive_command_guar...  // Indistinguishable!
```

When the unique part is:
```
destructive_command_guard_cc_1
destructive_command_guard_cc_2
```

## Fix Implementation

### 1. Smart Truncation (Middle Ellipsis)
```go
// TruncateMiddle keeps beginning and end, ellipsis in middle
func TruncateMiddle(s string, max int) string {
    if max <= 0 {
        return ""
    }
    runes := []rune(s)
    if len(runes) <= max {
        return s
    }

    ellipsis := "…"
    ellipsisLen := 1
    available := max - ellipsisLen

    // Keep more from the end (unique part) than beginning
    endChars := available * 2 / 3
    startChars := available - endChars

    if startChars < 3 {
        startChars = 3
    }
    if endChars < 3 {
        endChars = 3
    }

    return string(runes[:startChars]) + ellipsis + string(runes[len(runes)-endChars:])
}

// Result: "destru…_cc_16"
```

### 2. Common Prefix Detection
```go
// TruncateWithCommonPrefix for lists of similar strings
func TruncateWithCommonPrefix(items []string, maxWidth int) []string {
    if len(items) <= 1 {
        return truncateAll(items, maxWidth)
    }

    // Find common prefix
    prefix := items[0]
    for _, item := range items[1:] {
        prefix = commonPrefix(prefix, item)
    }

    // If significant common prefix, show abbreviated
    if len(prefix) > maxWidth/2 {
        // Show: "[prefix]…_cc_1" -> "[dcg]…_cc_1"
        abbrev := abbreviate(prefix)
        // ...
    }
    return truncateAll(items, maxWidth)
}
```

### 3. Numbered Suffix
```go
// For lists with common names, add distinguishing numbers
func renderListWithNumbers(items []string, maxWidth int) []string {
    counts := make(map[string]int)
    result := make([]string, len(items))

    for i, item := range items {
        truncated := TruncateMiddle(item, maxWidth-6) // Leave room for " (1/N)"
        counts[truncated]++
        if counts[truncated] > 1 {
            result[i] = truncated + fmt.Sprintf(" (%d)", counts[truncated])
        } else {
            result[i] = truncated
        }
    }
    return result
}
```

## Unit Tests Required
```go
func TestTruncateMiddle(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        input string
        max   int
        want  string
    }{
        {"destructive_command_guard_cc_16", 20, "destru…d_cc_16"},
        {"short", 20, "short"},
        {"abcdefghij", 7, "abc…ij"},
    }

    for _, tc := range tests {
        got := TruncateMiddle(tc.input, tc.max)
        if got != tc.want {
            t.Errorf("TruncateMiddle(%q, %d) = %q, want %q",
                tc.input, tc.max, got, tc.want)
        }
        if lipgloss.Width(got) > tc.max {
            t.Errorf("result width %d exceeds max %d",
                lipgloss.Width(got), tc.max)
        }
    }
}

func TestSimilarItemsDistinguishable(t *testing.T) {
    items := []string{
        "destructive_command_guard_cc_1",
        "destructive_command_guard_cc_2",
        "destructive_command_guard_cc_16",
    }

    truncated := truncateAll(items, 25)

    // All truncated items should be unique
    seen := make(map[string]bool)
    for _, item := range truncated {
        if seen[item] {
            t.Errorf("duplicate truncated item: %q", item)
        }
        seen[item] = true
    }
}
```

## E2E Test (VHS)
```tape
# truncation_test.tape
Set Width 120
Type "ntm dashboard --demo-data"
Sleep 2s

# With many similarly named items, they should be distinguishable
Screenshot truncation_distinguishable.png

Set Width 80
Sleep 1s
Screenshot truncation_narrow.png
```

## Verification
- [ ] TruncateMiddle preserves unique suffix
- [ ] Similar entries are visually distinguishable
- [ ] Panel width adjusts for content when possible
- [ ] Works with emoji and CJK in names","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:09:24.134632524Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:08:57.541500167Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:08:57.541445244Z","close_reason":"Implemented TruncateMiddle and TruncateMiddleWidth functions in internal/tui/layout/layout.go. Added comprehensive unit tests. Also fixed broken terminal package as part of this work.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qcn","depends_on_id":"bd-1ay2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:15:59.902274139Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qcn","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:41.541535156Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qdhp","title":"CRITICAL: Always send Enter twice after marching orders","description":"When sending marching orders to agents via tmux send-keys, Enter must be sent TWICE.

## The Problem
Agents appear to receive marching orders but never start processing them.

## Root Cause
A single Enter key press may not reliably submit the message in Claude Code / Codex CLI.
The message sits in the input buffer without being submitted.

## The Fix
After sending the marching orders text, ALWAYS send Enter twice with a small delay:

```bash
# Send marching orders (no Enter at end)
tmux send-keys -t "$session:1.$pane" "$msg"
sleep 1
# CRITICAL: Send Enter TWICE
tmux send-keys -t "$session:1.$pane" Enter
sleep 0.5
tmux send-keys -t "$session:1.$pane" Enter
```

## Code Locations to Fix
- internal/commands/spawn.go - sendMarchingOrders()
- internal/commands/send.go - sendToPane()
- Any function that sends text to agent panes

## Testing
After fix, verify with:
```bash
tmux capture-pane -t session:1.1 -p | tail -20
```
Should show agent actively processing, not waiting at input prompt.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-21T09:31:04.196222340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T09:31:04.196222340Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -467,7 +467,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-lo0ko","title":"Task: Add memory_mb field for process memory usage","description":"## What

Add `memory_mb` field showing the resident memory usage of the agent process.

## Why

Runaway processes can consume excessive memory, eventually causing OOM kills
or system slowdown. Early detection allows proactive intervention.

## Implementation

```go
func getProcessMemoryMB(pid int) (int, error) {
    data, err := os.ReadFile(fmt.Sprintf("/proc/%d/status", pid))
    if err != nil {
        return 0, err
    }
    
    for _, line := range strings.Split(string(data), "\n") {
        if strings.HasPrefix(line, "VmRSS:") {
            // Format: "VmRSS:    123456 kB"
            parts := strings.Fields(line)
            if len(parts) >= 2 {
                kb, _ := strconv.Atoi(parts[1])
                return kb / 1024, nil  // Convert to MB
            }
        }
    }
    return 0, nil
}
```

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "panes": [{
    "index": 2,
    "child_pid": 12346,
    "memory_mb": 450,
    "memory_warning": false  // true if > threshold
  }]
}
```

## Thresholds

- Normal: < 500 MB
- Warning: 500-1000 MB
- Critical: > 1000 MB

These thresholds should be configurable.

## Alternative: Total Process Tree Memory

For more accuracy, sum memory of child_pid and all its descendants:

```go
func getProcessTreeMemoryMB(pid int) int {
    total := getProcessMemoryMB(pid)
    children := getChildPIDs(pid)
    for _, child := range children {
        total += getProcessTreeMemoryMB(child)
    }
    return total
}
```

## Testing

- Unit test: Mock /proc reads
- Integration test: Verify against known process memory","notes":"Feature already implemented in status_enrich.go. getProcessMemoryMB function reads VmRSS from /proc/<pid>/status and enrichAgentStatus populates MemoryMB field.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:19:26.830905564Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T02:40:09.515349481Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T02:40:09.515258209Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lo0ko","depends_on_id":"bd-1g9wv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:19:26.835672001Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-lzvh2","title":"process_triage Integration: Bayesian Agent Health Monitoring","description":"Integrate process_triage (pt) for detecting stuck, abandoned, or zombie agents.

## Project Location
/dp/process_triage (Rust CLI)

## Problem Being Solved

Agents can become unresponsive:
- Waiting for API response that never comes
- Stuck in infinite loop
- Process crashed but pane still open
- Wedged on user input prompt

Without monitoring:
- Manually check each pane
- Agents consume resources doing nothing
- User doesn't know work has stopped

## What process_triage Does

Bayesian classification of process state:

### Classification Categories
- **useful**: Actively working, CPU/IO activity
- **waiting**: Legitimately waiting (network, user input)
- **idle**: Finished work, waiting for new task
- **stuck**: No progress for extended period
- **zombie**: Process exists but not running

### Signals Used
- CPU usage over time
- IO activity (read/write)
- Network activity (from rano integration)
- Output activity (pane output changes)
- Process state (running, sleeping, stopped)

### Key pt Commands
```bash
pt classify 1234         # Classify single PID
pt watch 1234,1235       # Continuous monitoring
pt report                # Status of all watched
```

## Integration Strategy for NTM

### 1. Monitor Agent Panes
For each agent pane:
- Get shell PID and child PIDs
- Feed to pt for monitoring
- React to classification changes

### 2. Alert on Stuck Agents
When pt classifies agent as stuck:
- Highlight in dashboard
- Optional: send notification
- Optional: auto-restart prompt

### 3. Robot Mode Commands
- --robot-agent-health: Get health status per agent

### 4. Dashboard Integration
Show agent health status:
- 🟢 useful/waiting
- 🟡 idle (>5 min)
- 🔴 stuck (>10 min)

## Considerations
- pt needs tuning for AI agent behavior
- Waiting for API is normal (long inference)
- Correlation with rano improves accuracy","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:20:49.078277818Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T07:20:49.111344193Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lzvh2","depends_on_id":"bd-99a2i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:20:49.111307393Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-mk1i5","title":"Tier 1: Safety & Reliability Integrations","description":"EPIC for Tier 1 integrations focused on immediate safety and reliability improvements.

## Why Tier 1 is Highest Priority

These three integrations address the most critical pain points in multi-agent workflows:

1. **DCG**: Prevents catastrophic mistakes. A single 'rm -rf /' or 'git push --force origin main' from any agent can destroy hours of work or worse. DCG blocks these at the command level before they execute.

2. **CAAM**: Rate limits are the #1 operational pain point. When Claude hits rate limits, you wait hours. CAAM has OAuth tokens for multiple accounts and can switch in <100ms, letting work continue immediately.

3. **caut**: Cost visibility prevents bill shock. 16+ providers tracked. Know when you're approaching limits before you hit them.

## Integration Approach

All three are Rust CLIs that work via subprocess invocation:
- DCG: Hook into PreToolUse (already exists in claude-code ecosystem)
- CAAM: Add --robot-switch-account command to NTM robot mode
- caut: Add --robot-quota-status and dashboard widget

## Success Metrics

- [ ] Zero destructive commands execute across managed agents (DCG)
- [ ] Rate limit recovery time < 1 minute (CAAM)
- [ ] Quota visibility in dashboard with alerts at 80% threshold (caut)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:43:27.049682004Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T06:43:27.079334148Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mk1i5","depends_on_id":"bd-3fg81","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:43:27.079285837Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-mk1i5","title":"Tier 1: Safety & Reliability Integrations","description":"EPIC for Tier 1 integrations focused on immediate safety and reliability improvements.

## Why Tier 1 is Highest Priority

These three integrations address the most critical pain points in multi-agent workflows:

1. **DCG**: Prevents catastrophic mistakes. A single 'rm -rf /' or 'git push --force origin main' from any agent can destroy hours of work or worse. DCG blocks these at the command level before they execute.

2. **CAAM**: Rate limits are the #1 operational pain point. When Claude hits rate limits, you wait hours. CAAM has OAuth tokens for multiple accounts and can switch in <100ms, letting work continue immediately.

3. **caut**: Cost visibility prevents bill shock. 16+ providers tracked. Know when you're approaching limits before you hit them.

## Integration Approach

All three are Rust CLIs that work via subprocess invocation:
- DCG: Hook into PreToolUse (already exists in claude-code ecosystem)
- CAAM: Add --robot-switch-account command to NTM robot mode
- caut: Add --robot-quota-status and dashboard widget

## Success Metrics

- [ ] Zero destructive commands execute across managed agents (DCG)
- [ ] Rate limit recovery time < 1 minute (CAAM)
- [ ] Quota visibility in dashboard with alerts at 80% threshold (caut)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:43:27.049682004Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T18:00:12.558020123Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T18:00:12.557947457Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mk1i5","depends_on_id":"bd-3fg81","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:43:27.079285837Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-n770","title":"Add YAML swarm config file - define entire swarm (agents, prompts, assignments) in one declarative file","description":"## Overview
Define entire swarm configuration in one declarative YAML file.

## Config Format (swarm.yaml)
```yaml
name: myproject-swarm
session: myproject

agents:
  claude: 10
  codex: 10  
  gemini: 4
  
options:
  no-user: true
  auto-assign: true
  
prompts:
  init: |
    Read AGENTS.md and register with Agent Mail.
    Check your inbox and respond to any messages.
    Use bv --robot-triage to find work.
  review: |
    Review code written by fellow agents...
  post-compaction: |
    Reread AGENTS.md. Use ultrathink.

assignment:
  source: bv
  limit: 24
  template: impl
  round-robin: false
  
monitor:
  stuck_threshold_seconds: 300
  auto_restart_stuck: true
  completion_patterns:
    - 'bead complete'
    - 'task finished'
```

## Commands
```bash
ntm swarm --config=swarm.yaml           # Start full swarm
ntm spawn --config=swarm.yaml           # Just spawn from config
ntm assign --config=swarm.yaml          # Use assignment settings
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parses YAML config format
- [ ] Validates required fields
- [ ] Supports all spawn options
- [ ] Supports prompt templates
- [ ] Supports assignment settings
- [ ] Supports monitor settings
- [ ] Clear error messages for invalid config

## Tests Required
- Unit test: YAML parsing and validation
- Unit test: Config defaults
- E2E test: Full swarm from config file","notes":"Closed as dropped: avoid new YAML format; reuse existing pipeline/recipe configs if declarative setup is needed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T08:40:47.613482061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:16:34.562867377Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T19:09:17.646542265Z","close_reason":"Dropped per canonical assign flow; redundant/overlapping entry points","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-n770","depends_on_id":"bd-2rqt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:19:39.439091708Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
